blocks.content: It wasn't long. It wasn't nothing. It was a real thing that happened, on the catwalk of the water tower on a flat grey November Saturday, and he let it happen, and somewhere in the middle of it he looked past her shoulder at the view and his eyes went without deciding to — straight to the field.
She was smiling. She was a good person, and that made it worse.
She turned back to the view. He looked at the field below and the houses on Magnolia Street, visible from up here if you knew where to look.
They climbed down without saying much. On the walk back, Brooke slipped her hand into his and he let her.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: This is fine. This is a normal thing. This is what people do.
He thought it all the way home.
